items.headline: SB 79 Overview: State Law on Housing Near Transit
items.summary: The Heritage Preservation Commission heard a presentation on California's Abundant and Affordable Homes Near Transit Act (SB 79, 2025). The law affects how local land-use decisions work for housing projects near transit corridors.

items.headline: 6-unit apartment building permitted on North 6th Street
items.summary: A new 6-unit apartment building is cleared to build at 445 N 6th St in the downtown core, adding rental housing to an infill location.

items.headline: Santa Clara renews Tourism Improvement District, contracts with Discover Santa Clara
items.summary: The city conducted a public hearing on renewing the Santa Clara Tourism Improvement District and approved an agreement with Silicon Valley/Santa Clara DMO, Inc. (doing business as Discover Santa Clara) to administer the district. The renewal was confirmed through majority protest proceedings.

items.headline: City defers Stadium Authority capital projects decision to later date
items.summary: The council postponed action on proposed FY 2027-28 Santa Clara Stadium Authority capital expense projects, deferring the decision from the June 9, 2026 meeting.

items.headline: City considers parcel map to merge four existing parcels
items.summary: The council held a public hearing on a vesting tentative parcel map (PLN25-00559) to consolidate four existing parcels, though the agenda excerpt does not provide details on the location or reason for the merger.

items.headline: Council to adopt park dedication for Measure E site
items.summary: The city council is scheduled to recommend adoption of an ordinance dedicating a portion of the Measure E site as parkland. The ordinance is exempt from environmental review under state CEQA regulations.

items.headline: Council to review Baylands Golf Links driving range expansion
items.summary: The council will review a financial feasibility study for expanding the driving range at Baylands Golf Links. The study will inform whether the expansion project moves forward.

items.headline: Planning Commission Studies Artificial Turf Policy
items.summary: The Planning Commission held a study session on June 22 to discuss an artificial turf policy. No vote or decision was recorded in the agenda.
